# deploy

<!-- archie:ai-start -->

> Kubernetes deployment artefacts for OpenMeter: two independently versioned Helm charts (openmeter, benthos-collector) published to GHCR OCI registry, plus a local kind cluster config for development. deploy/charts owns the shared helm-docs generation pipeline consumed by both charts.

## Patterns

**helm-docs via shared template** — Both chart READMEs are generated by helm-docs from deploy/charts/template.md and each chart's values.yaml comments. Never hand-edit a chart README.md. (`make -C deploy/charts docs  # regenerates both chart READMEs from template.md + values.yaml annotations`)
**OCI Helm registry distribution** — Charts are packaged and pushed to ghcr.io OCI registry on release tags, not a traditional index-based Helm repository. (`helm package deploy/charts/openmeter --version <VERSION> --destination build/helm`)
**Enabled-guard + configmap override for optional infrastructure** — Optional chart features (Kafka, ClickHouse, Redis bundled) use an enabled: bool guard in resource templates AND a corresponding override block in configmap.yaml. (`{{- if .Values.kafka.enabled }}...{{- end }} paired with kafka section in configmap.yaml`)

## Key Files

| File | Role | Watch For |
|------|------|-----------|
| `deploy/charts/Makefile` | Drives helm-docs README generation for both charts. | Adding a third chart requires a new README.tmpl.md and an update to this Makefile docs target. |
| `deploy/charts/template.md` | Shared helm-docs template consumed by both child charts. | Changes here affect both chart READMEs. Keep template generic — no chart-specific content. |
| `deploy/kind.yaml` | kind cluster config for local Kubernetes development with ingress port mappings (80/443). | Port mappings must match the Helm chart service/ingress defaults to avoid local routing mismatches. |
| `deploy/charts/openmeter/templates/configmap.yaml` | Renders the OpenMeter config.yaml from Helm values; all optional infrastructure blocks must have corresponding enabled guards. | Missing enabled guard in configmap.yaml for a new optional feature will inject config for disabled services. |

## Anti-Patterns

- Hand-editing a child chart README.md — it is generated by helm-docs from template.md and values.yaml comments; edits will be overwritten on next `make docs`
- Adding a third chart without a corresponding README.tmpl.md and Makefile update
- Storing shared Kubernetes manifests (CRDs, cluster-scoped RBAC) at deploy/charts level — each chart must be fully self-contained
- Publishing charts without aligning Chart.yaml appVersion with the container image tag in values.yaml

## Decisions

- **Shared template.md at deploy/charts level rather than duplicated per chart.** — Avoids README format drift between the two charts; single edit point for documentation structure.
- **OCI registry (ghcr.io) as the distribution channel, not a traditional Helm repository index.** — OCI registries provide content-addressable artefact storage and align with the container image distribution model already used for application images.

<!-- archie:ai-end -->
